intro: Athyrocarpus mathewsii, also known as Mathew's palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, native to the tropical forests of Western Australia. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, that have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Athyrocarpus mathewsii are responsible for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
